title: The political initiative belongs to PM Wickremesinghe and his  cabinet.If they can semi-normalise the distribution of fuel and gas in the coming week, they and the country will gain a much needed breathing space. If they fail, mass violence might break out.
body_lines: “Without gas, without kerosene oil, we can’t do anything. Last option what? Without food we are going to die. That will happen hundred percent.” ~ Mohammad Shazly a part-time chauffer, Colombo (Reuters – 20.5.2022

An old man walks down a road, snowy haired, thin to the point of gauntness, bespectacled. He clutches a white cane in one hand; the other steadies the gas cylinder he is bearing on his frail shoulders. That one picture, probably taken from a phone, portrays better than a thousand words or figures our current condition

The Rajapaksa-made crisis affects all Lankans, but not in the same way and not to the same degree. The greatest burden is borne not by the upper or the middle classes but by the old and the new poor. For them, the crisis is a battle for survival in the most literal sense. They have little time or patience for the political arguments which engages those of us for whom the crisis is not yet (and may never be) a matter of life and death. As a man who had been waiting in a Colombo queue for hours for cooking gas told the NDTV, “We don’t mind Ranil or Basil or whoever… We want solutions to our people. We don’t mind Ranil come back or Mahinda come back. We want solutions to our people. That’s it.”

Parliamentarian Mujibar Rahuman this week gave voice to the pain and despair of Colombo’s old and new poor. He also sounded a dire and accurate warning of the consequences of their anger. He asked the new Prime Minister about the steps taken to alleviate the plight of these most vulnerable communities. Ranil Wickremesinghe’s answer didn’t inspire confidence. He talked of the global food crisis and said the issue would be studied by a committee next week (hopefully not the committee headed by Vajira Abeywardane).

The impending global food crisis is real. There’s another global development the IMF warned about this week which Mr. Wickremesinghe and his motley crew of ministers should pay close attention to – greater social unrest caused mainly by steep increases in food and fuel prices. In Sri Lanka, massive price increases are compounded by a near total unavailability of items essential to the maintenance of modern life, especially in urban settings. Add to that the non-availability of fertiliser for this season leading to a collapse of agriculture and a resultant food shortage, and all the ingredients are present for the cauldron to boil over.

Ranil Wickremesinghe’s record of wantonly destroying every chance history handed him is unprecedented, his ability to self-destruct, taking down his side with him, legendary. Still, I hope he succeeds in pulling Sri Lanka out of the economic morass the Rajapaksas led her into. And I fear the consequences of his failure, most of which will have to be borne by those like the old man in the picture.

The economic crisis has morphed into a political crisis, but addressing the immediate economic needs of the masses – the shortages of fuel and cooking gas – cannot await the resolution of any of the political issues, be it sending Gota home or abolishing the executive presidency or holding elections (all of which are necessary).

A new credit line is the lifeline we need desperately; if it comes, it will come with conditions which we are in no position to refuse. As the old adage warned, beggars can’t be choosers, not if they want to live. Without dollars to pay for essential imports, the country will grind to a halt. Innumerable lives will be endangered in the most literal sense and the public that has borne much will say enough. The first signs of the impending outbreak are visible in the impromptu road closures and the stopping of fuel tankers. When a dam breaks, the water sweeps over everything in its path. That could be Sri Lanka, soon.
A group of online activists called a day of protest on Sunday, April 3rd. The Gotabaya-Mahinda-Basil regime, unnerved by the Mirihana incident, imposed a curfew.

For the first time in Lankan history, ordinary people ignored the curfew, pouring out into the streets in their thousands. Across the country, men, women, and children, entire families marched out of their homes, holding handmade placards, chanting slogans and singing. Nothing could dampen their enthusiasm, not the heavy police presence or the equally heavy thundershowers. By that night, the Galle Face was full of mostly middle class youth from Colombo and suburbs, demanding the president’s resignation. Faced by hundreds of thousands of curfew breakers, the police could do little. The curfew was not extended, and the emergency removed a couple of days later.

President Gotabaya Rajapaksa re-imposed the curfew, on May 9th, while SLPP thugs were attacking Gota-go-gama protestors. Across the nation, the curfew was ignored, this time not by peaceful protestors but by marauding mobs.

On May 10th, President Gotabaya called in the military. The next day, tanks rolled down streets, manned by fully armed and stony faced soldiers. The visuals would have chilled any democratic-minded citizen to the bone. As the military took over urban centres and rural towns, a silence fell. There was no public outcry; thousands of people did not pour into the streets in peaceful protests as they had done on April 3rd.

The difference in public reaction was made up of the counter-violence that broke out after the wanton attacks on GotaGoGama and MynaGoGama protest encampments. Had the reaction to the regime’s criminal onslaught been non-violent, had the streets been filled with peaceful protestors instead of rampaging mobs, the subsequent events might have taken a different turn. Gotabaya Rajapaksa prevailed because the mob violence gave him the opportunity to call in the troops.

By the time the tanks rolled in, the public was exhausted and fearful of both types of violence, and confused about the future. The feel-good factor that characterised the protests from the beginning was gone. That mood of felicity couldn’t have survived the Nittambuwa lynching. Though the full horror of that incident was not known (the parliamentarian and his protection officer had been beaten to death as the post-mortem revealed), the pictures of the bodies, stripped and lying on the road, were enough to cause a sense of shock and shame.

Perhaps a different outcome was possible. When an attempt was made – obviously by Rajapaksa agents – to ignite a religious conflagration in Negambo, the response from the larger protest movement was swift and unequivocal. This timely and decisive intervention was seminal in preventing Negambo from exploding. Had there been a similar response at least after the Nittambuwa lynching, the subsequent mob violence could have been avoided. The protestors could have retained the moral highground, and the regime deprived of an excuse to call the military in.

What if, instead of toppling vehicles and pushing the captured thugs into Beira Lake, (and stripping them), the protestors treated these Rajapaksa stooges with civility and handed them over either to the media or the police? Such an example could have set the tone for subsequent public reactions. Then again, these same protestors chased away Sajith Premadasa and Anura Kumara Dissanayake when they went to Galle Face immediately after the attack on GotaGoGama. Surely, if their presence was unwelcome, they could have been told so politely, and asked to leave? What are we, in the absence of such basic civility and tolerance?

A measured response didn’t happen, and the resultant fires burnt out the political component of mass anger. A nation of peaceful protestors was changed back into a nation of onlookers who watched mutely as the military took over public spaces. It was a time of a deadly political vacuum. The PM has resigned. There was no government, only a politically weakened President Gotabaya and the military, an institution still trusted by a majority of the people, according to the penultimate CPA survey. By accepting the premiership while Sajith Premadasa was dithering, Ranil Wickremesinghe might well have saved civilian rule. He may also have saved the Gotabaya presidency in the process. But then even a President Gotabaya is preferable to a military junta.
Mass political protests are finished for now. GotaGoGama survives, but mostly as a relic of an age of innocence lost forever.

The political initiative belongs to PM Wickremesinghe and his seriously underwhelming cabinet, though not for long. If they can semi-normalise the distribution of fuel and gas in the coming week, they and the country will gain a much needed breathing space. If they fail, mass violence might break out, not organised by any party, but stemming from the unbearable anguish and uncontainable rage of the people.
And once again, Sri Lanka will teeter on the edge of two interconnected abysses – mob rule and military rule, violent anarchy and violent dictatorship.

In his first public address as prime minister, Ranil Wickremesinghe compared the task he had taken upon himself to Grusha’s perilous journey on the rotten bridge across the glacier. There is another scene in Brecht’s iconic play that corresponds more exactly to the current Lankan condition. That comes in Act 5, when Azdek the judge orders the child Michael to be placed in the centre of a chalk circle and decrees that he will belong to the woman who pulls him out of the circle first.

Natella, the governor’s wife and the birth mother who abandoned the child pulls him out, twice. Grusha, the servant girl who saved him at the risk of her life, refuses to pull him, for she can’t bear to hurt him. Natella wants something from the child (access to her dead husband’s properties); Grusha wants something for the child, a life lived as a compassionate human being and not a brutal oppressor.

Sri Lanka is that child now, caught between many Natellas with no Grusha in sight. Unlike Michael, we bear considerable responsibility for our own plight. Gota must go home, for he is the chief author of our tragedy. But he sits right because he can cling to the massive mandate we gave him. We elected a silly little showman with a Hitler complex and now we are saddled with him.
Rejecting all 225 parliamentarians is a fad now, but other than national list members, everyone else of that despised tribe were also elected by us.

Chamal Rajapaksa rightly faulted brother Mahinda for not bowing out of politics after his defeat at the 2015 Presidential election. Had he done so, there would have been no Mahinda Sulanga, no SLPP, no President Gotabaya. Sri Lanka would have muddled along as she did for 72 years, with some successes and many failures. Rajapaksa greed brought us all to this pass, but it was we the people who gave them the chance to wreck Sri Lanka. Understanding our own culpability is necessary to prevent a repeat. If we don’t realise and accept the role we played in our own downfall, we will elect another set of venal incompetents at the next election.

Gota won’t go and there’s no constitutional path to oust him fast, so the only option is to defang him via a 21st Amendment and to hold a referendum on the presidential system in tandem with the next parliamentary election. But there’s a kind of a use value in Gota refusing to go home. So long as he clings to the presidency, people will remember the true authors of their unbearable suffering. If he’s gone, the role of the Rajapaksas will be forgotten gradually, and the blame heaped on whoever is governing, be it Ranil, Sajith, Anura or anyone else.

After a few months of non-Rajapaksa rule, Mahinda Rajapaksa will claim that he had a plan, but couldn’t implement it due to national and international conspiracies. And quite a few of those who are now clamouring for Gota’s departure now will scratch their collective heads then muttering, That is also true (Ekath aththa).

There might not be another Mahinda Sulanga but a Mahinda Madanala (Mahinda Breeze) is a distinct possibility under such circumstances. Allowing a defanged Gotabaya Rajapaksa to cling to the presidency until the worst of the crisis is over might make political sense, given Lankan public’s well known capacity to swing from one extreme to another in short oder.
In the final act of the Caucasian Chalk Circle, a song reflecting Grusha’s hopes for the child ends with the line that she wants him to fear darkness but not the night.
Last night, the house of an IOC fuel station owner in Kekirawa was torched, allegedly by people who could not purchase fuel. That is our darkness.
Yesterday, an internet post reported that in Walgama, residents provided those in a fuel queue overnight with biscuits, tea, and even breakfast.

We need dollars urgently. We also need to find and cling to ordinary everyday decency, kindness, and empathy that are in us. We can either become Grushas and be kind to each other or turn into Natellas and tear each other apart.

si.title: දේශපාලන මුලපිරීම අගමැති වික්‍රමසිංහට සහ ඔහුගේ කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලයට අයත් වේ. ඉදිරි සතියේදී ඉන්ධන සහ ගෑස් බෙදා හැරීම අර්ධ වශයෙන් සාමාන්‍යකරණය කළ හැකි නම්, ඔවුන්ට සහ රටට අත්‍යවශ්‍ය හුස්ම ගැනීමේ අවකාශයක් ලැබෙනු ඇත. ඒවා අසාර්ථක වුවහොත් මහා ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය ඇති විය හැක.
body_lines: “ගෑස් නැතිව, භූමිතෙල් නැතිව අපිට කිසිම දෙයක් කරන්න බැහැ. අවසාන විකල්පය කුමක්ද? කෑම නැතිව අපි මැරෙනවා. ඒක සියයට සියයක්ම සිදුවේවි.” ~ මොහොමඩ් ෂාස්ලි අර්ධ කාලීන රියදුරෙක්, කොළඹ (රොයිටර් - 20.5.2022

මහලු මිනිසෙක්, හිමෙන් වැසුණු හිසකෙස් ඇති, සිහින්ව පෙනෙන තරම් සිහින්, කණ්නාඩි පැළඳ පාරක ගමන් කරයි. ඔහු එක් අතකින් සුදු සැරයටියක් අල්ලාගෙන සිටියි; අනෙක ඔහු දුර්වල උරහිස් මත දරා සිටින ගෑස් සිලින්ඩරය ස්ථාවර කරයි. එම එක් පින්තූරයක්, සමහරවිට දුරකථනයකින් ගන්නා ලද, වචන දහසකට වඩා හොඳින් නිරූපණය කරයි, නැතහොත් අපගේ වර්තමාන තත්ත්වය නිරූපණය කරයි

රාජපක්ෂ විසින් ඇති කරන ලද අර්බුදය සියලු ලාංකිකයන්ට බලපාන නමුත් එකම ආකාරයකින් නොව එකම මට්ටමකින් නොවේ. ලොකුම බර දරන්නේ ඉහළ හෝ මධ්‍යම පන්තිකයින් නොව පැරණි සහ නව දුප්පතුන් විසිනි. ඔවුන්ට, අර්බුදය යනු වඩාත් වචනාර්ථයෙන් පැවැත්ම සඳහා වන සටනකි. අර්බුදය තවමත් ජීවිතය හා මරණය පිළිබඳ කාරණයක් නොවන (සහ කිසි විටෙක නොවිය හැකි) දේශපාලන තර්ක සඳහා ඔවුන්ට කාලය හෝ ඉවසීම අඩුය. ඉවුම් පිහුම් ගෑස් සඳහා පැය ගණන් කොළඹ පෝලිමේ සිටි මිනිසෙක් NDTV වෙත පැවසුවේ, “අපිට රනිල් හෝ බැසිල් හෝ කවුරු වුණත් කමක් නැහැ... අපට අවශ්‍ය අපේ ජනතාවට විසඳුම්. අපිට රනිල් ආවත් මහින්ද ආවත් කමක් නැහැ. අපිට ඕන අපේ ජනතාවට විසඳුම්. ඒක තමයි."

පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී මුජිබර් රහුමාන් මේ සතියේ කොළඹ පැරණි සහ නව දුප්පතුන්ගේ වේදනාව සහ බලාපොරොත්තු සුන්වීම වෙනුවෙන් හඬක් නැගුවේය. ඔවුන්ගේ කෝපයේ ප්‍රතිවිපාක පිළිබඳ දරුණු හා නිවැරදි අනතුරු ඇඟවීමක් ද ඔහු කළේය. වඩාත් අවදානමට ලක්විය හැකි මෙම ප්‍රජාවන්ගේ දුක්ඛිත තත්ත්වයන් සමනය කිරීමට ගෙන ඇති පියවර පිළිබඳව ඔහු නව අග්‍රාමාත්‍යවරයාගෙන් විමසා සිටියේය. රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහගේ පිළිතුර විශ්වාසයක් ඇති කළේ නැත. ඔහු ගෝලීය ආහාර අර්බුදය ගැන කතා කළ අතර ප්‍රශ්නය ලබන සතියේ කමිටුවක් මගින් අධ්‍යයනය කරනු ඇතැයි පැවසීය (වජිර අබේවර්ධනගේ ප්‍රධානත්වයෙන් යුත් කමිටුව නොවේ).

ඉදිරියේ ඇති ගෝලීය ආහාර අර්බුදය සැබෑ ය. මේ සතියේදී IMF අනතුරු ඇඟවූ තවත් ගෝලීය වර්ධනයක් ඇත, එය වික්‍රමසිංහ මහතා සහ ඔහුගේ හැඩකාර අමාත්‍ය මණ්ඩලය දැඩි අවධානයක් යොමු කළ යුතුය - ප්‍රධාන වශයෙන් ආහාර සහ ඉන්ධන මිල ඉහළ යාම නිසා ඇති වන විශාල සමාජ නොසන්සුන්තාව. ශ්‍රී ලංකාව තුළ, විශේෂයෙන්ම නාගරික පරිසරය තුළ, නූතන ජීවිතය පවත්වාගෙන යාමට අත්‍යවශ්‍ය ද්‍රව්‍ය සම්පූර්ණයෙන්ම නොලැබීම නිසා දැවැන්ත මිල වැඩිවීම් සංකීර්ණ වේ. මෙම කන්නයේ පොහොර නොලැබීම කෘෂිකර්මාන්තය කඩා වැටීමට හා ආහාර හිඟයකට තුඩු දෙන අතර, වට්ටක්කා උණු කිරීම සඳහා සියලු අමුද්රව්ය තිබේ.

රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහගේ ඉතිහාසය ඔහුට ලබා දුන් සෑම අවස්ථාවක්ම අනවසරයෙන් විනාශ කිරීමේ වාර්තාව පෙර නොවූ විරූ, ස්වයං-විනාශ කිරීමේ ඔහුගේ හැකියාව, ඔහු සමඟ ඔහුගේ පැත්ත ඉවත් කිරීම, පුරාවෘත්තයකි. කෙසේ වෙතත්, රාජපක්ෂලා විසින් ශ්‍රී ලංකාව ගෙන ගිය ආර්ථික අර්බුදයෙන් ශ්‍රී ලංකාව ගොඩ ගැනීමට ඔහු සමත් වනු ඇතැයි මම බලාපොරොත්තු වෙමි. ඔහුගේ අසාර්ථක වීමේ ප්‍රතිවිපාක ගැන මම බිය වෙමි, එයින් බොහෝමයක් පින්තූරයේ සිටින මහලු මිනිසා වැනි අයට දරාගත යුතුය.

ආර්ථික අර්බුදය දේශපාලන අර්බුදයක් දක්වා වර්ධනය වී ඇති නමුත් මහජනතාවගේ ක්ෂනික ආර්ථික අවශ්‍යතා සපුරාලීම - ඉන්ධන සහ පිසින ගෑස් හිඟය - ගෝඨා ගෙදර යැවීම හෝ විධායක ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමය අහෝසි කිරීම හෝ වේවා දේශපාලන ප්‍රශ්න කිසිවක් විසඳන තෙක් බලා සිටිය නොහැක. මැතිවරණ පැවැත්වීම (ඒ සියල්ල අවශ්‍ය වේ).

නව ණය රේඛාවක් යනු අපට අත්‍යාවශ්‍ය ජීවන රේඛාවයි; එය පැමිණෙන්නේ නම්, එය අපට ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළ නොහැකි කොන්දේසි සහිතව පැමිණෙනු ඇත. පැරණි කියමන අනතුරු ඇඟවූ පරිදි, යාචකයින්ට තෝරා ගන්නන් විය නොහැක, ඔවුන්ට ජීවත් වීමට අවශ්‍ය නම් නොවේ. අත්‍යවශ්‍ය ආනයන සඳහා ගෙවීමට ඩොලර් නොමැතිව රට ඇඹරෙනු ඇත. අසංඛ්‍යාත ජීවිත ඉතාමත්ම වචනාර්ථයෙන් අනතුරට පත් වනු ඇති අතර බොහෝ දේ දරා සිටින මහජනතාව ප්‍රමාණවත් යැයි කියනු ඇත. හදිසියේ මාර්ග වසා දැමීම් සහ ඉන්ධන නැව් නැවැත්වීම තුළ ඉදිරියේදී ඇති විය හැකි වසංගතයේ පළමු සලකුණු දක්නට ලැබේ. වේල්ලක් කැඩී ගිය විට ජලය එහි ගමන් කරන සෑම දෙයක්ම අතුගා දමයි. එය ඉක්මනින්ම ශ්‍රී ලංකාව විය හැක.
අන්තර්ජාල ක්‍රියාකාරීන් පිරිසක් අප්‍රේල් 3 ඉරිදා විරෝධතා දිනයක් කැඳවූහ. මිරිහාන සිද්ධියෙන් නොසන්සුන් වූ ගෝඨාභය-මහින්ද-බැසිල් පාලනය ඇඳිරි නීතිය පැනවීය.

ලංකා ඉතිහාසයේ පළමු වතාවට සාමාන්‍ය ජනතාව ඇඳිරි නීතිය නොසලකා හරිමින් දහස් ගණනින් පාරට බැස්සේය. රට පුරා, පිරිමින්, කාන්තාවන් සහ ළමයින්, මුළු පවුලම තම නිවෙස්වලින් පිටතට ගොස්, අතින් සාදන ලද පුවරු අල්ලාගෙන, සටන් පාඨ කියමින් සහ ගායනා කළහ. කිසිවකට ඔවුන්ගේ උද්යෝගය අඩු කළ නොහැකි විය, අධික පොලිස් සිටීම හෝ ඒ හා සමානව ගිගුරුම් සහිත වැසි නොවේ. එදින රාත්‍රිය වන විට ගාලු මුවදොර පිරී තිබුණේ ජනාධිපතිවරයාට ඉල්ලා අස්වන ලෙස බලකරමින් කොළඹ සහ තදාසන්න ප්‍රදේශවලින් පැමිණි මධ්‍යම පාන්තික තරුණයන්ගෙන් බහුතරයකිනි. ලක්ෂ සංඛ්‍යාත ඇඳිරි නීතිය කඩ කරන්නන් හමුවේ පොලිසියට කළ හැකි වූයේ අල්ප වශයෙනි. ඇඳිරි නීතිය දීර්ඝ නොකළ අතර හදිසි නීතිය දින දෙකකට පසුව ඉවත් කරන ලදී.

ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණේ මැරයන් ගෝඨා-ගොගම විරෝධතාකරුවන්ට ප්‍රහාර එල්ල කරද්දී, ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ මහතා මැයි 9 වැනිදා යළි ඇඳිරි නීතිය පැනවීය. රට පුරා ඇඳිරි නීතිය නොසලකා හරින ලද්දේ මෙවර සාමකාමී විරෝධතාකරුවන් විසින් නොව මැර කල්ලි විසිනි.

මැයි 10 වෙනිදා ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය හමුදාව කැඳෙව්වා. ඊළඟ දවසේ, සම්පූර්ණ ආයුධ සන්නද්ධ සහ ගල් මුහුණැති සොල්දාදුවන් විසින් මෙහෙයවන ලද ටැංකි වීදි දිගේ පෙරළී ගියේය. මෙම දර්ශන මගින් ඕනෑම ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී මනසක් ඇති පුරවැසියෙකුව තුට්ටුවකට මායිම් කිරීමට ඉඩ තිබුණි. මිලිටරිය නාගරික මධ්‍යස්ථාන සහ ගම්බද නගර අත්පත් කර ගැනීමත් සමඟ නිශ්ශබ්දතාවයක් ඇති විය. මහජන විරෝධයක් නොතිබුණි; අප්‍රේල් 3 වැනිදා කළා වගේ දහස් ගණන් ජනතාව සාමකාමී උද්ඝෝෂණවලට පාරට බැස්සේ නැහැ.

මහජන ප්‍රතිචාරයේ වෙනස ගොඩනැගුනේ ගෝඨගෝගම සහ මයිනාගෝගම විරෝධතා කඳවුරුවලට අනවසරයෙන් එල්ල වූ ප්‍රහාරවලින් පසුව ඇති වූ ප්‍රති-ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය මගිනි. පාලන තන්ත්‍රයේ සාපරාධී ප්‍රහාරයට ප්‍රතිචාරය ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වයෙන් තොර වූයේ නම්, මැර කල්ලිවලට පහර දීම වෙනුවට සාමකාමී විරෝධතාකරුවන්ගෙන් වීදි පිරී ගියේ නම්, පසුකාලීන සිදුවීම් වෙනස් මුහුණුවරක් ගැනීමට ඉඩ තිබුණි. ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ ජය ගත්තේ මැර ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය ඔහුට හමුදාව කැඳවීමට අවස්ථාව ලබා දුන් බැවිනි.

ටැංකි පෙරළෙන කාලය වන විට, මහජනතාව වෙහෙසට පත්ව සිටි අතර ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය දෙවර්ගයටම බිය වූ අතර අනාගතය ගැන ව්‍යාකූල විය. ආරම්භයේ සිටම විරෝධතා සංලක්ෂිත වූ හැඟීම්-හොඳ සාධකය පහව ගියේය. එම ප්‍රීතිමත් මනෝභාවය නිට්ටඹුව ඝාතනයෙන් බේරීමට නොහැකි විය. එම සිදුවීමේ සම්පූර්ණ භීෂණය නොදැන සිටියද (පශ්චාත් මරණ පරීක්‍ෂණයේදී හෙළිවූ පරිදි පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රීවරයා සහ ඔහුගේ ආරක්‍ෂක නිලධාරියා පහරදී ඝාතනය කර ඇති බව) සිරුරු නිරුවත් කර මහමග වැටී ඇති ඡායාරූප ඒ පිළිබඳ හැඟීමක් ඇති කිරීමට ප්‍රමාණවත් විය. කම්පනය සහ ලැජ්ජාව.

සමහරවිට වෙනස් ප්රතිඵලය විය හැකිය. මීගමුවේ ආගමික ගිනිදැල් ඇවිලවීමට - පැහැදිලිවම රාජපක්ෂ ඒජන්තයින් විසින් - උත්සාහයක් ගත් විට, විශාල විරෝධතා ව්‍යාපාරයේ ප්‍රතිචාරය වේගවත් සහ නිසැක විය. මෙම කාලෝචිත සහ තීරණාත්මක මැදිහත්වීම මීගමුව පිපිරීම වැලැක්වීමට මූලික විය. අඩුම තරමින් නිට්ටඹුවේ ඝාතන සිද්ධියෙන් පසුවවත් එවැනි ප්‍රතිචාරයක් ලැබුණේ නම් ඉන් පසුව ඇති වූ මැර ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය වළක්වා ගත හැකිව තිබුණි. විරෝධතාකරුවන්ට සදාචාරාත්මක උච්චස්ථානය රඳවා ගත හැකිව තිබූ අතර, හමුදාව කැඳවීමට නිදහසට කරුණක් පාලන තන්ත්‍රයට අහිමි විය.

වාහන පෙරළීම සහ අල්ලා ගත් මැරයන් බේරේ වැවට තල්ලු කිරීම වෙනුවට (ඔවුන් නිරුවත් කිරීම) වෙනුවට උද්ඝෝෂකයින් මෙම රාජපක්ෂ ගෝලබාලයින්ට සභ්‍යත්වයෙන් සළකා ඔවුන්ව මාධ්‍යයට හෝ පොලිසියට භාර දුන්නේ නම් කුමක් කළ යුතුද? එවැනි උදාහරණයක් පසුකාලීන මහජන ප්‍රතිචාර සඳහා තානය සැකසිය හැකිය. ඉන්පසුව නැවතත් මෙම විරෝධතාකරුවන්ම සජිත් ප්‍රේමදාස සහ අනුර කුමාර දිසානායක ගෝඨාගෝගමට ප්‍රහාරය එල්ල වූ විගස ගාලුමුවදොරට යන විට එළවා දැමුවේය. නිසැකවම, ඔවුන්ගේ පැමිණීම අකමැත්තක් නම්, ඔවුන්ට එතරම් ආචාරශීලීව පවසා ඉවත්ව යන ලෙස පැවසිය හැකිද? එවැනි මූලික ශිෂ්ටාචාරයක් සහ ඉවසීමක් නොමැති විට අප කුමක් ද?

මනින ලද ප්‍රතිචාරයක් සිදු නොවූ අතර, එහි ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස ඇති වූ ගින්න මහජන කෝපයේ දේශපාලන සංරචකය පුළුස්සා දැමීය. සාමකාමී විරෝධතාකරුවන්ගෙන් යුත් ජාතියක් නැවත හමුදාව පොදු අවකාශයන් අත්පත් කර ගන්නා විට නිහඬව බලා සිටි නරඹන්නන්ගේ ජාතියක් බවට පත් කරන ලදී. එය මාරාන්තික දේශපාලන රික්තකයක් පැවති කාලයකි. අගමැති ඉල්ලා අස්වී ඇත. ආණ්ඩුවක් තිබුණේ නැත, දේශපාලනික වශයෙන් දුර්වල වූ ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය සහ හමුදාව පමණක්, තවමත් බහුතර ජනතාවක් විශ්වාස කරන ආයතනයක්, අවසාන CPA සමීක්ෂණයට අනුව. සජිත් ප්‍රේමදාස මහතා වියරු වැටී සිටියදී අගමැතිකම භාරගැනීමෙන් රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ මහතා සිවිල් පාලනය බේරා ගැනීමට ඉඩ තිබුණි. ඔහු එම ක්‍රියාවලියේදී ගෝඨාභය ජනාධිපතිකමද බේරා ගන්නට ඇත. නමුත් එවිට ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය වුවද හමුදා ජුන්ටාවකට වඩා සුදුසුය.
මහජන දේශපාලන විරෝධතා දැනට අවසන්. GotaGoGama නොනැසී පවතී, නමුත් බොහෝ විට සදහටම අහිමි වූ අහිංසක යුගයක ධාතුවක් ලෙස.

දේශපාලන මුලපිරීම දිගු කලක් නොවුනත් අගමැති වික්‍රමසිංහට සහ ඔහුගේ බරපතල ලෙස යටපත් වී ඇති කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලයට අයත් වේ. ඉදිරි සතියේදී ඔවුන්ට ඉන්ධන සහ ගෑස් බෙදා හැරීම අර්ධ වශයෙන් සාමාන්‍යකරණය කළ හැකි නම්, ඔවුන්ට සහ රටට අවශ්‍ය හුස්ම ගැනීමේ ඉඩක් ලැබෙනු ඇත. ඔවුන් අසමත් වුවහොත්, මහජන ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය පැන නැගිය හැකි අතර, එය කිසිදු පක්ෂයක් විසින් සංවිධානය නොකළ නමුත්, මිනිසුන්ගේ දරාගත නොහැකි වේදනාවෙන් සහ පාලනය කළ නොහැකි කෝපයෙන් පැන නගී.
නැවත වරක්, ශ්‍රී ලංකාව අන්තර් සම්බන්ධිත අගාධ දෙකක - මැර පාලනය සහ හමුදා පාලනය, ප්‍රචණ්ඩ අරාජිකත්වය සහ ප්‍රචණ්ඩ ආඥාදායකත්වය - අද්දර ගමන් කරනු ඇත.

අග්‍රාමාත්‍යවරයා ලෙස සිය ප්‍රථම ප්‍රසිද්ධ දේශනයේදී රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ තමා විසින් භාරගත් කාර්යය සසඳන ලද්දේ ග්ලැසියරය හරහා දිරාගිය පාලම මතින් ගෲෂාගේ අනතුරුදායක ගමනටය. බ්‍රෙෂ්ට්ගේ ප්‍රතිමූර්තිය නාට්‍යයේ වත්මන් ලංකා තත්ත්වයට වඩාත් ගැලපෙන තවත් දර්ශනයක් තිබේ. එය 5 වන පනතේ එන, Azdek විනිසුරුවරයා මයිකල් දරුවා හුණු රවුමක මධ්‍යයේ තබන ලෙස නියෝග කරන අතර ඔහු මුලින්ම රවුමෙන් පිටතට ඇද ගන්නා කාන්තාවට ඔහු අයිති වන බවට නියෝග කරයි.

ආණ්ඩුකාරවරයාගේ බිරිඳ වන නටෙල්ලා සහ දරුවා අතහැර දැමූ මව ඔහුව දෙවරක් පිටතට ඇද දමයි. තම ජීවිතය පරදුවට තබා ඔහුව බේරාගත් ගෲෂා නම් සේවිකාව, ඔහුට රිදවීම දරාගත නොහැකි නිසා ඔහුව ඇද ගැනීම ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කරයි. නැටෙල්ලාට දරුවාගෙන් යමක් අවශ්යයි (ඇගේ මියගිය සැමියාගේ දේපල වෙත ප්රවේශය); ගෲෂාට අවශ්‍ය වන්නේ දරුවාට යමක්, ම්ලේච්ඡ මනුෂ්‍යයෙකු ලෙස ජීවත් වන ජීවිතයක් මිස ම්ලේච්ඡ පීඩකයෙකු නොවේ.

ගෲෂා නොපෙනෙන බොහෝ නැටල්ලන් අතරට හසු වූ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව දැන් එම දරුවා ය. මයිකල් මෙන් නොව, අපගේම දුක්ඛිත තත්වයට අපි සැලකිය යුතු වගකීමක් දරයි. අපේ ඛේදවාචකයේ ප්‍රධාන කතුවරයා ගෝඨා නිසා ගෙදර යා යුතුයි. නමුත් අපි ඔහුට ලබා දුන් දැවැන්ත ජනවරමේ එල්ලී සිටිය හැකි නිසා ඔහු නිවැරදිව වාඩි වී සිටී. අපි හිට්ලර් සංකීර්ණයක් සහිත මෝඩ කුඩා සංදර්ශනකරුවෙකු තෝරාගෙන දැන් අපි ඔහු සමඟ අසුන්ගෙන සිටිමු.
මන්ත‍්‍රීවරුන් 225 දෙනාම ප‍්‍රතික්ෂේප කිරීම දැන් විලාසිතාවක් නමුත් ජාතික ලැයිස්තු මන්ත‍්‍රීවරුන් හැරුණු විට එම ගර්හාවට ලක් වූ ගෝත‍්‍රයේ අනෙක් සියල්ලෝද අප විසින් තේරී පත් වූවෝය.

2015 ජනාධිපතිවරණ පරාජයෙන් පසු දේශපාලනයෙන් හිස නොනැමීම ගැන චමල් රාජපක්ෂ මහතා මහින්ද සහෝදරයාට දොස් කීවේය. ඔහු එසේ කළා නම්, මහින්ද සුළඟක්, ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණක්, ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය නොවන්නට තිබුණා. වසර 72ක් ඇය කළාක් මෙන් ශ්‍රී ලංකාව යම් යම් ජයග්‍රහණ සහ බොහෝ අසාර්ථකවීම් සමඟ මඩ ගැසෙන්නට ඇත. රාජපක්ෂලාගේ කෑදරකම අපි හැමෝම මේ තත්වෙට ගෙනාවා, නමුත් ලංකාව විනාශ කරන්න ඔවුන්ට අවස්ථාව දුන්නේ අපියි. පුනරාවර්තනය වීම වැලැක්වීම සඳහා අපගේම වරදකාරිත්වය අවබෝධ කර ගැනීම අවශ්ය වේ. අපේම කඩාවැටීමට අප කළ කාර්යභාරය අවබෝධ කර නොගන්නේ නම්, ඊළඟ මැතිවරණයේදී අපි තවත් වල් අදක්ෂයින් පිරිසක් තෝරා ගනු ඇත.

ගෝඨා නොයනු ඇති අතර ඔහුව ඉක්මනින් නෙරපා හැරීමට ව්‍යවස්ථාපිත මාර්ගයක් නොමැත, එබැවින් එකම විකල්පය වන්නේ 21 වැනි සංශෝධනයක් හරහා ඔහුව අපකීර්තියට පත් කර ඉදිරි පාර්ලිමේන්තු මැතිවරණයට සමගාමීව ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමය පිළිබඳ ජනමත විචාරණයක් පැවැත්වීමයි. එහෙත් ගෝඨා ගෙදර යාම ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කිරීම තුළ යම් ආකාරයක භාවිත වටිනාකමක් තිබේ. ඔහු ජනාධිපතිකමේ එල්ලී සිටින තාක් කල්, මිනිසුන්ට දරාගත නොහැකි දුක් වේදනාවල සැබෑ කතුවරුන් සිහිපත් වනු ඇත. ඔහු නැති වුවහොත් රාජපක්ෂලාගේ ක්‍රියාකලාපය ක්‍රමක්‍රමයෙන් අමතක වී, රනිල්, සජිත්, අනුර හෝ කවුරුන් හෝ ආණ්ඩු කළත් වරද පැටවෙනු ඇත.

මාස කිහිපයක රාජපක්ෂ නොවන පාලනයෙන් පසු, මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ තමාට සැලසුමක් තිබූ නමුත් ජාතික හා ජාත්‍යන්තර කුමන්ත්‍රණ හේතුවෙන් එය ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීමට නොහැකි වූ බව කියනු ඇත. ඒ වගේම දැන් ගෝඨාගේ නික්මයාමට කෑගහන කීප දෙනෙක්ම සාමූහික ඔළුව කසනවා, ඒකත් ඇත්ත (එකත් ඇත්ත) කියලා.

තවත් මහින්ද සුළඟක් නොතිබිය හැකි නමුත් මහින්ද මඩනලයක් (මහින්ද බ්‍රීස්) එවැනි තත්වයන් යටතේ පැහැදිලි විය හැකිය. කෙටි කාලයකින් එක් අන්තයක සිට තවත් අන්තයකට පැද්දීමට ලාංකීය මහජනතාවගේ සුප්‍රසිද්ධ හැකියාව සැලකිල්ලට ගෙන, අර්බුදයේ නරකම කාලය අවසන් වන තුරු, අපකීර්තියට පත් ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂට ජනාධිපති ධුරයේ එල්ලී සිටීමට ඉඩ දීම දේශපාලන අර්ථයක් විය හැකිය.
Caucasian Chalk Circle හි අවසාන ක්‍රියාවේදී, Grusha ගේ දරුවා පිළිබඳ බලාපොරොත්තු පිළිබිඹු කරන ගීතයක් අවසන් වන්නේ ඔහු අඳුරට බිය වීමට ඇයට අවශ්‍ය නමුත් රාත්‍රියට බිය වීමට නොවේ.
ඊයේ රාත්‍රියේ කැකිරාව ප්‍රදේශයේ IOC ඉන්ධන පිරවුම්හලක හිමිකරුවෙකුගේ නිවසකට ඉන්ධන මිලදී ගැනීමට නොහැකි වූ පිරිසක් විසින් ගිනිතබා තිබේ. ඒක තමයි අපේ අඳුර.
ඊයේ අන්තර්ජාල සටහනකින් වාර්තා වූයේ වල්ගම ප්‍රදේශයේ නිවැසියන් රාත්‍රියේ ඉන්ධන පෝලිමේ සිටි අයට බිස්කට්, තේ සහ උදේ ආහාරය පවා ලබා දුන් බවයි.

අපිට ඉක්මනින් ඩොලර් අවශ්‍යයි. අප තුළ පවතින සාමාන්‍ය එදිනෙදා විනීතභාවය, කරුණාව සහ අනුකම්පාව සොයාගෙන ඒවාට ඇලී සිටිය යුතුය. අපට එක්කෝ Grushas බවට පත් වී එකිනෙකාට කාරුණික විය හැකිය, නැතහොත් Natellas බවට පත් වී එකිනෙකා ඉරා දැමිය හැකිය.

ta.title: அரசியல் முன்முயற்சி பிரதமர் விக்ரமசிங்க மற்றும் அவரது அமைச்சரவைக்கு சொந்தமானது. வரும் வாரத்தில் எரிபொருள் மற்றும் எரிவாயு விநியோகத்தை அவர்களால் அரைகுறையாக இயல்பாக்க முடிந்தால், அவர்களும் நாடும் மிகவும் தேவையான சுவாச இடத்தைப் பெறுவார்கள். அவர்கள் தோல்வியுற்றால், பாரிய வன்முறை வெடிக்கலாம்.
body_lines: "எரிவாயு இல்லாமல், மண்ணெண்ணெய் இல்லாமல், எங்களால் எதுவும் செய்ய முடியாது. கடைசி விருப்பம் என்ன? உணவின்றி சாகப் போகிறோம். அது நூறு சதவிகிதம் நடக்கும்." ~ முகமது ஷாஸ்லி ஒரு பகுதி நேர ஓட்டுநர், கொழும்பு (ராய்ட்டர்ஸ் – 20.5.2022

ஒரு முதியவர் ஒரு சாலையில் நடந்து செல்கிறார், பனி முடியுடன், மெல்லியதாக, கண்கண்ணாடியுடன். அவர் ஒரு கையில் ஒரு வெள்ளை கரும்பு; மற்றொன்று அவர் பலவீனமான தோள்களில் சுமந்து கொண்டிருக்கும் எரிவாயு உருளையை நிலைநிறுத்துகிறது. அந்த ஒரு படம், அநேகமாக ஃபோனில் இருந்து எடுக்கப்பட்டது, ஆயிரம் வார்த்தைகளை விட சிறப்பாக சித்தரிக்கிறது அல்லது நமது தற்போதைய நிலையைக் காட்டுகிறது

ராஜபக்ச உருவாக்கிய நெருக்கடி அனைத்து இலங்கையர்களையும் பாதிக்கிறது, ஆனால் அதே வழியில் அல்ல, ஒரே அளவில் அல்ல. மிகப் பெரிய சுமை மேல்தட்டு அல்லது நடுத்தர வர்க்கத்தினரால் சுமக்கப்படவில்லை, மாறாக பழைய மற்றும் புதிய ஏழைகளால் சுமக்கப்படுகிறது. அவர்களைப் பொறுத்தவரை, நெருக்கடி என்பது மிகவும் நேரடி அர்த்தத்தில் உயிர்வாழ்வதற்கான ஒரு போர். நெருக்கடி இன்னும் வாழ்க்கை மற்றும் இறப்பு பிரச்சினையாக இல்லாத (ஒருபோதும் இருக்கக்கூடாது) அரசியல் வாதங்களுக்கு அவர்களுக்கு சிறிது நேரம் அல்லது பொறுமை இல்லை. சமையல் எரிவாயுவுக்காக பல மணிநேரம் கொழும்பு வரிசையில் காத்திருந்த ஒருவர் NDTV இடம் கூறியது போல், “எங்களுக்கு ரணில் அல்லது பசில் அல்லது யாராக இருந்தாலும் பரவாயில்லை… எங்கள் மக்களுக்கு தீர்வுகள் வேண்டும். ரணில் மீண்டு வந்தாலும் மகிந்த வந்தாலும் எங்களுக்கு கவலையில்லை. எமது மக்களுக்கு தீர்வுகள் வேண்டும். அவ்வளவுதான்."

பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் முஜிபர் ரகுமான் இந்த வாரம் கொழும்பின் பழைய மற்றும் புதிய ஏழைகளின் வலி மற்றும் விரக்திக்காக குரல் கொடுத்தார். அவர்களின் கோபத்தின் விளைவுகளைப் பற்றிய கடுமையான மற்றும் துல்லியமான எச்சரிக்கையையும் அவர் ஒலித்தார். மிகவும் பாதிக்கப்படக்கூடிய இந்த சமூகங்களின் அவலத்தைப் போக்க எடுக்கப்பட்ட நடவடிக்கைகள் குறித்து அவர் புதிய பிரதமரிடம் கேட்டறிந்தார். ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்கவின் பதில் நம்பிக்கையை ஏற்படுத்தவில்லை. உலகளாவிய உணவு நெருக்கடி பற்றி அவர் பேசினார், மேலும் இந்த பிரச்சினையை அடுத்த வாரம் ஒரு குழு ஆய்வு செய்யும் (வஜிர அபேவர்தன தலைமையிலான குழு அல்ல என்று நம்புகிறேன்).

வரவிருக்கும் உலகளாவிய உணவு நெருக்கடி உண்மையானது. இந்த வாரம் சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியம் எச்சரித்துள்ள மற்றொரு உலகளாவிய முன்னேற்றம் உள்ளது, இது திரு. விக்கிரமசிங்கவும் அவரது அமைச்சர்கள் குழுவும் உன்னிப்பாகக் கவனிக்க வேண்டும் - முக்கியமாக உணவு மற்றும் எரிபொருள் விலைகளில் செங்குத்தான அதிகரிப்பால் ஏற்படும் சமூக அமைதியின்மை. இலங்கையில், குறிப்பாக நகர்ப்புற அமைப்புகளில், நவீன வாழ்க்கையைப் பராமரிப்பதற்கு அத்தியாவசியமான பொருட்கள் கிட்டத்தட்ட மொத்தமாக கிடைக்காததால் பாரிய விலை உயர்வுகள் சேர்ந்துள்ளன. இந்த சீசனுக்கான உரம் கிடைக்காததால் விவசாயம் பொய்த்து, உணவுப் பற்றாக்குறை ஏற்பட்டு, கொப்பரை கொதிநிலைக்கு தேவையான அனைத்து பொருட்களும் உள்ளன.

ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க வரலாறு தனக்குக் கிடைத்த ஒவ்வொரு வாய்ப்பையும் வேண்டுமென்றே அழித்த சாதனை முன்னெப்போதும் இல்லாதது, தன்னைத்தானே அழித்துக்கொள்ளும் திறன், அவருடன் தனது பக்கத்தை எடுத்துக்கொள்வது, பழம்பெரும். இருப்பினும், ராஜபக்சேக்கள் இலங்கையை இட்டுச் சென்ற பொருளாதாரச் சிக்கலில் இருந்து இலங்கையை மீட்டெடுப்பதில் அவர் வெற்றி பெறுவார் என நம்புகிறேன். மேலும் அவரது தோல்வியின் விளைவுகளைப் பற்றி நான் அஞ்சுகிறேன், அவற்றில் பெரும்பாலானவை படத்தில் உள்ள முதியவர் போன்றவர்களால் தாங்கப்பட வேண்டும்.

பொருளாதார நெருக்கடி ஒரு அரசியல் நெருக்கடியாக உருவெடுத்துள்ளது, ஆனால் மக்களின் உடனடி பொருளாதாரத் தேவைகளை நிவர்த்தி செய்வது - எரிபொருள் மற்றும் சமையல் எரிவாயு தட்டுப்பாடு - கோத்தாவை வீட்டிற்கு அனுப்புவது அல்லது நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி முறையை நீக்குவது அல்லது எந்தவொரு அரசியல் பிரச்சினைக்கும் தீர்வுக்காக காத்திருக்க முடியாது. தேர்தல்களை நடத்துதல் (அவை அனைத்தும் அவசியம்).

ஒரு புதிய கடன் வரி என்பது நமக்கு மிகவும் தேவைப்படும் உயிர்நாடி; வந்தால், நாம் மறுக்க முடியாத நிபந்தனைகளுடன் வரும். பழைய பழமொழி எச்சரித்தபடி, பிச்சைக்காரர்கள் தேர்வு செய்பவர்களாக இருக்க முடியாது, அவர்கள் வாழ விரும்பினால் அல்ல. அத்தியாவசிய இறக்குமதிகளுக்கு டாலர்கள் இல்லாமல், நாடு ஸ்தம்பித்துவிடும். எண்ணிலடங்கா உயிர்கள் மிகவும் நேரடி அர்த்தத்தில் ஆபத்தில் இருக்கும் மற்றும் நிறைய தாங்கிய பொதுமக்கள் போதும் என்று கூறுவார்கள். வரவிருக்கும் வெடிப்பின் முதல் அறிகுறிகள் முன்கூட்டியே சாலை மூடல்கள் மற்றும் எரிபொருள் டேங்கர்களை நிறுத்துவதில் தெரியும். ஒரு அணை உடைந்தால், தண்ணீர் அதன் பாதையில் உள்ள அனைத்தையும் துடைக்கிறது. அது விரைவில் இலங்கையாக இருக்கலாம்.

ஆன்லைன் ஆர்வலர்கள் குழு ஒன்று ஏப்ரல் 3 ஆம் தேதி ஞாயிற்றுக்கிழமை எதிர்ப்பு தினத்திற்கு அழைப்பு விடுத்துள்ளது. மிரிஹான சம்பவத்தால் பதற்றமடைந்த கோட்டாபய-மஹிந்த-பசில் ஆட்சி, ஊரடங்குச் சட்டத்தை அமுல்படுத்தியது.

இலங்கை வரலாற்றில் முதல் தடவையாக ஊரடங்குச் சட்டத்தை புறக்கணித்த சாதாரண மக்கள் ஆயிரக்கணக்கில் வீதிகளில் இறங்கினர். நாடு முழுவதும், ஆண்கள், பெண்கள் மற்றும் குழந்தைகள், முழு குடும்பங்களும் தங்கள் வீடுகளை விட்டு வெளியேறி, கையால் செய்யப்பட்ட பதாகைகளை ஏந்தியபடி, முழக்கங்களை எழுப்பினர் மற்றும் பாடினர். பலத்த போலீஸ் பிரசன்னமோ அல்லது அதே அளவு பலத்த இடியுடன் கூடிய மழையோ எதுவும் அவர்களின் உற்சாகத்தைக் குறைக்கவில்லை. அன்றிரவு, ஜனாதிபதியின் பதவி விலகலைக் கோரி கொழும்பு மற்றும் புறநகர்ப் பகுதிகளைச் சேர்ந்த பெரும்பாலான நடுத்தர வர்க்க இளைஞர்களால் காலி முகத்திடல் நிரம்பியிருந்தது. நூறாயிரக்கணக்கான ஊரடங்குச் சட்டத்தை மீறுபவர்களை எதிர்கொண்டதால், காவல்துறையால் எதுவும் செய்ய முடியவில்லை. ஊரடங்கு உத்தரவு நீட்டிக்கப்படவில்லை, மேலும் சில நாட்களுக்குப் பிறகு அவசரநிலை நீக்கப்பட்டது.

கோட்டா-கோ-கம எதிர்ப்பாளர்கள் மீது SLPP குண்டர்கள் தாக்குதல் நடத்திய போது, ​​ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ, மே 9 ஆம் தேதி மீண்டும் ஊரடங்குச் சட்டத்தை அமுல்படுத்தினார். நாடு முழுவதும், ஊரடங்கு உத்தரவு புறக்கணிக்கப்பட்டது, இந்த முறை அமைதியான எதிர்ப்பாளர்களால் அல்ல, ஆனால் கொள்ளை கும்பல்களால்.

மே 10 ஆம் திகதி ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய இராணுவத்திற்கு அழைப்பு விடுத்தார். அடுத்த நாள், டாங்கிகள் தெருக்களில் உருண்டன, முழு ஆயுதம் ஏந்திய மற்றும் கல்லால் ஆன முகம் கொண்ட வீரர்கள் இருந்தனர். இந்தக் காட்சிகள் ஜனநாயக மனப்பான்மை கொண்ட எந்த ஒரு குடிமகனையும் குளிர்விக்கும். இராணுவம் நகர்ப்புற மையங்களையும் கிராமப்புற நகரங்களையும் கைப்பற்றியதால், ஒரு அமைதி நிலவியது. பொதுமக்கள் கூச்சல் இல்லை; ஏப்ரல் 3-ம் தேதி நடத்திய அமைதியான போராட்டங்களில் ஆயிரக்கணக்கான மக்கள் தெருக்களில் இறங்கவில்லை.

கோட்டகோகாமா மற்றும் மைனகோகாமா எதிர்ப்பு முகாம்கள் மீதான விரும்பத்தகாத தாக்குதல்களுக்குப் பிறகு வெடித்த எதிர்-வன்முறையால் பொதுமக்களின் எதிர்வினை வித்தியாசமானது. ஆட்சியின் கிரிமினல் தாக்குதலுக்கான எதிர்வினை வன்முறையற்றதாக இருந்திருந்தால், தெருக்கள் அமைதியான எதிர்ப்பாளர்களால் நிரம்பியிருந்தால், அதற்குப் பதிலாக கும்பல் கும்பலைத் தாக்கியிருந்தால், அடுத்தடுத்த நிகழ்வுகள் வேறுவிதமாக மாறியிருக்கலாம். கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச வெற்றி பெற்றார், ஏனெனில் கும்பல் வன்முறை அவருக்கு படையினரை அழைக்கும் வாய்ப்பை வழங்கியது.

தொட்டிகள் உருளும் நேரத்தில், பொதுமக்கள் சோர்வடைந்து, இரண்டு வகையான வன்முறைகளுக்கும் பயந்து, எதிர்காலத்தைப் பற்றி குழப்பமடைந்தனர். ஆரம்பத்திலிருந்தே எதிர்ப்புகளை வகைப்படுத்திய உணர்வு-நல்ல காரணி இல்லாமல் போய்விட்டது. அந்த மகிழ்ச்சியான மனநிலை நிட்டம்புவ படுகொலையில் இருந்து தப்பித்திருக்க முடியாது. அந்தச் சம்பவத்தின் முழுப் பயங்கரம் தெரியாவிட்டாலும் (பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினரும் அவரது பாதுகாப்பு அதிகாரியும் அடித்துக் கொல்லப்பட்டது பிரேதப் பரிசோதனையில் தெரியவந்துள்ளது) உடல்கள், உரித்து உடைக்கப்பட்டு சாலையில் கிடக்கும் படங்களே போதுமானதாக இருந்தது. அதிர்ச்சி மற்றும் அவமானம்.

ஒருவேளை வேறு முடிவு சாத்தியமாகலாம். நீர்கொழும்பில் ஒரு மதக் கலவரத்தைத் தூண்டுவதற்கு - வெளிப்படையாக ராஜபக்ச முகவர்களால் - ஒரு முயற்சி மேற்கொள்ளப்பட்டபோது, ​​பெரிய எதிர்ப்பு இயக்கத்தின் பதில் விரைவானது மற்றும் சந்தேகத்திற்கு இடமின்றி இருந்தது. இந்த சரியான நேரத்தில் மற்றும் தீர்க்கமான தலையீடு நெகம்போ வெடிப்பதைத் தடுப்பதில் முதன்மையானது. குறைந்த பட்சம் நிட்டம்புவ படுகொலைகளுக்குப் பின்னராவது இதேபோன்ற பிரதிபலிப்பு இருந்திருந்தால், அடுத்தடுத்த கும்பல் வன்முறைகளைத் தவிர்த்திருக்கலாம். எதிர்ப்பாளர்கள் தார்மீக உயர்நிலையைத் தக்க வைத்துக் கொள்ள முடியும், மேலும் இராணுவத்தை உள்ளே அழைப்பதற்கான காரணத்தை ஆட்சி இழந்தது.

வாகனங்களை கவிழ்த்து, பிடிபட்ட குண்டர்களை பெய்ரா ஏரியில் தள்ளுவதற்குப் பதிலாக, (அவர்களை அகற்றி) போராட்டக்காரர்கள் இந்த ராஜபக்ஷ கைக்கூலிகளை நாகரீகமாக நடத்தி, ஊடகங்களிடம் அல்லது காவல்துறையிடம் ஒப்படைத்தால் என்ன செய்வது? அத்தகைய உதாரணம் அடுத்தடுத்த பொது எதிர்வினைகளுக்கு தொனியை அமைத்திருக்கலாம். மீண்டும், சஜித் பிரேமதாச மற்றும் அநுரகுமார திஸாநாயக்க ஆகியோர், கோட்டகோகம தாக்குதலுக்குப் பின்னர் உடனடியாக காலி முகத்திடலுக்குச் சென்றபோது, ​​இதே ஆர்ப்பாட்டக்காரர்கள் அவர்களை விரட்டியடித்தனர். நிச்சயமாக, அவர்களின் வருகை விரும்பத்தகாததாக இருந்தால், அவர்களை மிகவும் பணிவாகச் சொல்லி, வெளியேறச் சொல்ல முடியுமா? அத்தகைய அடிப்படை நாகரிகமும் சகிப்புத்தன்மையும் இல்லாத நிலையில் நாம் என்ன?

அளவிடப்பட்ட பதில் நடக்கவில்லை, அதன் விளைவாக ஏற்பட்ட நெருப்பு வெகுஜன கோபத்தின் அரசியல் கூறுகளை எரித்தது. அமைதியான போராட்டக்காரர்களின் தேசம், பொது இடங்களை இராணுவம் கையகப்படுத்துவதை ஊமையாகப் பார்க்கும் பார்வையாளர்களின் தேசமாக மீண்டும் மாற்றப்பட்டது. அது ஒரு கொடிய அரசியல் வெற்றிடத்தின் காலம். பிரதமர் பதவி விலகினார். இறுதிக்கட்ட CPA கணக்கெடுப்பின்படி, எந்த அரசாங்கமும் இல்லை, அரசியல் ரீதியாக பலவீனமான ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய மற்றும் இராணுவம் மட்டுமே, பெரும்பான்மை மக்களால் இன்னும் நம்பப்படும் ஒரு நிறுவனம். சஜித் பிரேமதாச தவித்துக்கொண்டிருந்த வேளையில் பிரதமர் பதவியை ஏற்றுக்கொண்டதன் மூலம் ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க சிவில் ஆட்சியைக் காப்பாற்றியிருக்கலாம். இந்த நடவடிக்கையில் அவர் கோட்டாபய ஜனாதிபதி பதவியையும் காப்பாற்றியிருக்கலாம். ஆனால் ஒரு ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய கூட இராணுவ ஆட்சியை விட விரும்பத்தக்கவர்.
வெகுஜன அரசியல் போராட்டங்கள் இப்போதைக்கு முடிவுக்கு வந்துள்ளன. கோட்டாகோகாமா உயிர் பிழைத்திருக்கிறார், ஆனால் பெரும்பாலும் அப்பாவித்தனமான ஒரு யுகத்தின் நினைவுச்சின்னமாக நிரந்தரமாக இழந்தார்.

இந்த அரசியல் முன்முயற்சி, நீண்ட காலமாக இல்லாவிட்டாலும், பிரதமர் விக்கிரமசிங்க மற்றும் அவரது தீவிரமான கீழ்நிலை அமைச்சரவைக்கு சொந்தமானது. வரவிருக்கும் வாரத்தில் எரிபொருள் மற்றும் எரிவாயு விநியோகத்தை அரை-சாதாரணமாக்க முடிந்தால், அவர்களும் நாடும் மிகவும் தேவையான சுவாச இடத்தைப் பெறுவார்கள். அவர்கள் தோல்வியுற்றால், எந்தக் கட்சியாலும் ஒழுங்கமைக்கப்படாமல் வெகுஜன வன்முறை வெடிக்கலாம், ஆனால் மக்களின் தாங்க முடியாத வேதனை மற்றும் அடக்க முடியாத ஆத்திரத்தில் இருந்து உருவாகலாம்.
மீண்டும், இலங்கை இரண்டு ஒன்றோடொன்று இணைக்கப்பட்ட படுகுழிகளின் விளிம்பில் தத்தளிக்கும் - கும்பல் ஆட்சி மற்றும் இராணுவ ஆட்சி, வன்முறை அராஜகம் மற்றும் வன்முறை சர்வாதிகாரம்.

ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க, பிரதமராக பதவியேற்றதன் மூலம் தனது முதல் பொது உரையில், தான் மேற்கொண்ட பணியை பனிப்பாறையின் குறுக்கே அழுகிய பாலத்தில் க்ருஷா மேற்கொண்ட ஆபத்தான பயணத்துடன் ஒப்பிட்டார். ப்ரெக்ட்டின் சின்னமான நாடகத்தில் தற்போதைய இலங்கையின் நிலைமைக்கு மிகவும் பொருத்தமான மற்றொரு காட்சி உள்ளது. அது சட்டம் 5 இல் வருகிறது, அஸ்டெக் நீதிபதி குழந்தை மைக்கேலை ஒரு சுண்ணாம்பு வட்டத்தின் மையத்தில் வைக்க உத்தரவிட்டார் மற்றும் அவரை முதலில் வட்டத்திலிருந்து வெளியே இழுக்கும் பெண்ணுக்குச் சொந்தமானவர் என்று ஆணையிடுகிறார்.

குழந்தையைக் கைவிட்ட ஆளுநரின் மனைவியும் பெற்ற தாயுமான நாடெல்லா அவரை இரண்டு முறை வெளியே இழுக்கிறார். தன் உயிரைப் பணயம் வைத்து அவனைக் காப்பாற்றிய வேலைக்காரியான க்ருஷா, அவனைத் துன்புறுத்துவதைத் தாங்க முடியாமல் அவனை இழுக்க மறுக்கிறாள். நாடெல்லா குழந்தையிடமிருந்து ஏதாவது விரும்புகிறார் (அவரது இறந்த கணவரின் சொத்துக்களை அணுகலாம்); க்ருஷா குழந்தைக்கு ஏதாவது வேண்டும், இரக்கமுள்ள மனிதனாக வாழ வேண்டும், மிருகத்தனமான அடக்குமுறையாளராக அல்ல.

க்ருஷா இல்லாத பல நாட்டெல்லாக்களுக்கு இடையில் சிக்கிய அந்தக் குழந்தைதான் இப்போது இலங்கை. மைக்கேலைப் போலல்லாமல், எங்கள் சொந்த அவலநிலைக்கு நாங்கள் கணிசமான பொறுப்பை ஏற்கிறோம். கோதா வீட்டிற்குச் செல்ல வேண்டும், ஏனென்றால் அவர் எங்கள் சோகத்தின் முக்கிய ஆசிரியர். ஆனால் அவர் சரியாக அமர்ந்திருக்கிறார், ஏனென்றால் நாங்கள் அவருக்கு வழங்கிய மகத்தான ஆணையை அவர் ஒட்டிக்கொள்ள முடியும். நாங்கள் ஒரு ஹிட்லர் வளாகத்துடன் ஒரு வேடிக்கையான சிறிய ஷோமேனைத் தேர்ந்தெடுத்தோம், இப்போது நாங்கள் அவருடன் சேணமாக இருக்கிறோம்.
225 பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர்களையும் நிராகரிப்பது இப்போது ஒரு பேஷன், ஆனால் தேசியப் பட்டியல் உறுப்பினர்களைத் தவிர, அந்த இழிவான பழங்குடியினரின் மற்ற அனைவரும் எங்களால் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்டவர்கள்.

2015 ஆம் ஆண்டு ஜனாதிபதித் தேர்தலில் தோல்வியடைந்ததன் பின்னர், அரசியலில் இருந்து ஒதுங்கிக் கொள்ளாத சகோதரர் மஹிந்தவை சமல் ராஜபக்ச சரியாகக் குற்றம் சாட்டினார். அப்படிச் செய்திருந்தால் மஹிந்த சுலங்கவோ, ஸ்ரீலங்கா பொதுஜன பெரமுனவோ, ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபயவோ இருந்திருக்க மாட்டார்கள். சில வெற்றிகள் மற்றும் பல தோல்விகளுடன் 72 வருடங்கள் செய்தது போல் இலங்கை குழப்பத்தில் இருந்திருக்கும். ராஜபக்சே பேராசை எங்களையெல்லாம் இந்த நிலைக்கு கொண்டு வந்தது, ஆனால் இலங்கையை நாசமாக்குவதற்கு அவர்களுக்கு வாய்ப்பளித்தவர்கள் நாங்கள்தான். மீண்டும் நிகழாமல் தடுக்க நமது சொந்த குற்றத்தைப் புரிந்துகொள்வது அவசியம். நமது வீழ்ச்சியில் நாம் ஆற்றிய பங்கை உணர்ந்து ஏற்றுக்கொள்ளாவிட்டால், அடுத்த தேர்தலில் திறமையற்ற மற்றொரு தொகுதியை நாம் தேர்ந்தெடுப்போம்.

கோட்டா செல்ல மாட்டார், அவரை வெளியேற்றுவதற்கு அரசியலமைப்பு ரீதியான பாதை எதுவும் இல்லை, எனவே அவரை 21 வது திருத்தத்தின் மூலம் சிதைப்பதும் அடுத்த பாராளுமன்றத் தேர்தலுடன் இணைந்து ஜனாதிபதி முறைமையில் பொதுவாக்கெடுப்பு நடத்துவதும் ஒரே வழி. ஆனால் கோதா வீட்டுக்குப் போக மறுப்பதில் ஒருவித உபயோக மதிப்பு இருக்கிறது. அவர் ஜனாதிபதி பதவியில் ஒட்டிக்கொண்டிருக்கும் வரை, மக்கள் தாங்க முடியாத துன்பத்தின் உண்மையான ஆசிரியர்களை நினைவில் வைத்திருப்பார்கள். அவர் மறைந்தால், ராஜபக்ஷக்களின் பங்கு படிப்படியாக மறக்கப்பட்டு, ரணில், சஜித், அனுர அல்லது வேறு யாராக இருந்தாலும், யார் ஆட்சி செய்தாலும் பழி சுமத்தப்படும்.

ராஜபக்ச அல்லாத ஆட்சியின் சில மாதங்களுக்குப் பிறகு, மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷ தன்னிடம் ஒரு திட்டம் இருப்பதாகக் கூறுவார், ஆனால் தேசிய மற்றும் சர்வதேச சதித்திட்டங்களால் அதை செயல்படுத்த முடியவில்லை. இப்போது கோட்டா வெளியேற வேண்டும் என்று கூக்குரலிடுபவர்களில் சிலர், அதுவும் உண்மைதான் (ஏகத் அத்தா) என்று முணுமுணுப்பார்கள்.

இன்னொரு மகிந்த சுளங்க இருக்கக்கூடாது ஆனால் ஒரு மஹிந்த மதனல (மஹிந்த தென்றல்) அத்தகைய சூழ்நிலையில் ஒரு தனித்துவமான சாத்தியமாகும். சீர்குலைக்கப்பட்ட கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச, நெருக்கடியின் மோசமான நிலை முடியும் வரை ஜனாதிபதி பதவியில் ஒட்டிக்கொள்ள அனுமதிப்பது அரசியல் அர்த்தமுள்ளதாக இருக்கலாம், குறுகிய காலத்தில் ஒரு தீவிரத்திலிருந்து இன்னொரு தீவிரத்திற்கு ஊசலாடும் இலங்கை மக்களின் நன்கு அறியப்பட்ட திறனைக் கருத்தில் கொண்டு.

காகசியன் சுண்ணாம்பு வட்டத்தின் இறுதிச் செயலில், குழந்தை மீதான க்ருஷாவின் நம்பிக்கையைப் பிரதிபலிக்கும் ஒரு பாடல், அவர் இருளைப் பற்றி பயப்பட வேண்டும், ஆனால் இரவைப் பற்றி பயப்படக்கூடாது என்ற வரியுடன் முடிகிறது.
நேற்றிரவு கெகிராவையில் உள்ள ஐஓசி எரிபொருள் நிரப்பு நிலைய உரிமையாளரின் வீடு எரிபொருளை கொள்வனவு செய்ய முடியாதவர்களால் எரிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. அதுதான் நமது இருள்.
நேற்று, வால்கமவில், குடியிருப்பாளர்கள் பிஸ்கட், தேநீர் மற்றும் காலை உணவைக் கூட ஒரே இரவில் எரிபொருள் வரிசையில் நின்றவர்களுக்கு வழங்கியதாக ஒரு இணைய இடுகை தெரிவிக்கிறது.

எங்களுக்கு அவசரமாக டாலர்கள் தேவை. நம்மில் இருக்கும் சாதாரண அன்றாட ஒழுக்கம், இரக்கம் மற்றும் பச்சாதாபம் ஆகியவற்றைக் கண்டுபிடித்து ஒட்டிக்கொள்ள வேண்டும். நாம் ஒன்று க்ருஷாக்களாக மாறி ஒருவருக்கொருவர் அன்பாக இருக்கலாம் அல்லது நாடெல்லாக்களாக மாறி ஒருவரையொருவர் பிரிக்கலாம்.

en.title: The political initiative belongs to PM Wickremesinghe and his  cabinet.If they can semi-normalise the distribution of fuel and gas in the coming week, they and the country will gain a much needed breathing space. If they fail, mass violence might break out.
body_lines: “Without gas, without kerosene oil, we can’t do anything. Last option what? Without food we are going to die. That will happen hundred percent.” ~ Mohammad Shazly a part-time chauffer, Colombo (Reuters – 20.5.2022

An old man walks down a road, snowy haired, thin to the point of gauntness, bespectacled. He clutches a white cane in one hand; the other steadies the gas cylinder he is bearing on his frail shoulders. That one picture, probably taken from a phone, portrays better than a thousand words or figures our current condition

The Rajapaksa-made crisis affects all Lankans, but not in the same way and not to the same degree. The greatest burden is borne not by the upper or the middle classes but by the old and the new poor. For them, the crisis is a battle for survival in the most literal sense. They have little time or patience for the political arguments which engages those of us for whom the crisis is not yet (and may never be) a matter of life and death. As a man who had been waiting in a Colombo queue for hours for cooking gas told the NDTV, “We don’t mind Ranil or Basil or whoever… We want solutions to our people. We don’t mind Ranil come back or Mahinda come back. We want solutions to our people. That’s it.”

Parliamentarian Mujibar Rahuman this week gave voice to the pain and despair of Colombo’s old and new poor. He also sounded a dire and accurate warning of the consequences of their anger. He asked the new Prime Minister about the steps taken to alleviate the plight of these most vulnerable communities. Ranil Wickremesinghe’s answer didn’t inspire confidence. He talked of the global food crisis and said the issue would be studied by a committee next week (hopefully not the committee headed by Vajira Abeywardane).

The impending global food crisis is real. There’s another global development the IMF warned about this week which Mr. Wickremesinghe and his motley crew of ministers should pay close attention to – greater social unrest caused mainly by steep increases in food and fuel prices. In Sri Lanka, massive price increases are compounded by a near total unavailability of items essential to the maintenance of modern life, especially in urban settings. Add to that the non-availability of fertiliser for this season leading to a collapse of agriculture and a resultant food shortage, and all the ingredients are present for the cauldron to boil over.

Ranil Wickremesinghe’s record of wantonly destroying every chance history handed him is unprecedented, his ability to self-destruct, taking down his side with him, legendary. Still, I hope he succeeds in pulling Sri Lanka out of the economic morass the Rajapaksas led her into. And I fear the consequences of his failure, most of which will have to be borne by those like the old man in the picture.

The economic crisis has morphed into a political crisis, but addressing the immediate economic needs of the masses – the shortages of fuel and cooking gas – cannot await the resolution of any of the political issues, be it sending Gota home or abolishing the executive presidency or holding elections (all of which are necessary).

A new credit line is the lifeline we need desperately; if it comes, it will come with conditions which we are in no position to refuse. As the old adage warned, beggars can’t be choosers, not if they want to live. Without dollars to pay for essential imports, the country will grind to a halt. Innumerable lives will be endangered in the most literal sense and the public that has borne much will say enough. The first signs of the impending outbreak are visible in the impromptu road closures and the stopping of fuel tankers. When a dam breaks, the water sweeps over everything in its path. That could be Sri Lanka, soon.
A group of online activists called a day of protest on Sunday, April 3rd. The Gotabaya-Mahinda-Basil regime, unnerved by the Mirihana incident, imposed a curfew.

For the first time in Lankan history, ordinary people ignored the curfew, pouring out into the streets in their thousands. Across the country, men, women, and children, entire families marched out of their homes, holding handmade placards, chanting slogans and singing. Nothing could dampen their enthusiasm, not the heavy police presence or the equally heavy thundershowers. By that night, the Galle Face was full of mostly middle class youth from Colombo and suburbs, demanding the president’s resignation. Faced by hundreds of thousands of curfew breakers, the police could do little. The curfew was not extended, and the emergency removed a couple of days later.

President Gotabaya Rajapaksa re-imposed the curfew, on May 9th, while SLPP thugs were attacking Gota-go-gama protestors. Across the nation, the curfew was ignored, this time not by peaceful protestors but by marauding mobs.

On May 10th, President Gotabaya called in the military. The next day, tanks rolled down streets, manned by fully armed and stony faced soldiers. The visuals would have chilled any democratic-minded citizen to the bone. As the military took over urban centres and rural towns, a silence fell. There was no public outcry; thousands of people did not pour into the streets in peaceful protests as they had done on April 3rd.

The difference in public reaction was made up of the counter-violence that broke out after the wanton attacks on GotaGoGama and MynaGoGama protest encampments. Had the reaction to the regime’s criminal onslaught been non-violent, had the streets been filled with peaceful protestors instead of rampaging mobs, the subsequent events might have taken a different turn. Gotabaya Rajapaksa prevailed because the mob violence gave him the opportunity to call in the troops.

By the time the tanks rolled in, the public was exhausted and fearful of both types of violence, and confused about the future. The feel-good factor that characterised the protests from the beginning was gone. That mood of felicity couldn’t have survived the Nittambuwa lynching. Though the full horror of that incident was not known (the parliamentarian and his protection officer had been beaten to death as the post-mortem revealed), the pictures of the bodies, stripped and lying on the road, were enough to cause a sense of shock and shame.

Perhaps a different outcome was possible. When an attempt was made – obviously by Rajapaksa agents – to ignite a religious conflagration in Negambo, the response from the larger protest movement was swift and unequivocal. This timely and decisive intervention was seminal in preventing Negambo from exploding. Had there been a similar response at least after the Nittambuwa lynching, the subsequent mob violence could have been avoided. The protestors could have retained the moral highground, and the regime deprived of an excuse to call the military in.

What if, instead of toppling vehicles and pushing the captured thugs into Beira Lake, (and stripping them), the protestors treated these Rajapaksa stooges with civility and handed them over either to the media or the police? Such an example could have set the tone for subsequent public reactions. Then again, these same protestors chased away Sajith Premadasa and Anura Kumara Dissanayake when they went to Galle Face immediately after the attack on GotaGoGama. Surely, if their presence was unwelcome, they could have been told so politely, and asked to leave? What are we, in the absence of such basic civility and tolerance?

A measured response didn’t happen, and the resultant fires burnt out the political component of mass anger. A nation of peaceful protestors was changed back into a nation of onlookers who watched mutely as the military took over public spaces. It was a time of a deadly political vacuum. The PM has resigned. There was no government, only a politically weakened President Gotabaya and the military, an institution still trusted by a majority of the people, according to the penultimate CPA survey. By accepting the premiership while Sajith Premadasa was dithering, Ranil Wickremesinghe might well have saved civilian rule. He may also have saved the Gotabaya presidency in the process. But then even a President Gotabaya is preferable to a military junta.
Mass political protests are finished for now. GotaGoGama survives, but mostly as a relic of an age of innocence lost forever.

The political initiative belongs to PM Wickremesinghe and his seriously underwhelming cabinet, though not for long. If they can semi-normalise the distribution of fuel and gas in the coming week, they and the country will gain a much needed breathing space. If they fail, mass violence might break out, not organised by any party, but stemming from the unbearable anguish and uncontainable rage of the people.
And once again, Sri Lanka will teeter on the edge of two interconnected abysses – mob rule and military rule, violent anarchy and violent dictatorship.

In his first public address as prime minister, Ranil Wickremesinghe compared the task he had taken upon himself to Grusha’s perilous journey on the rotten bridge across the glacier. There is another scene in Brecht’s iconic play that corresponds more exactly to the current Lankan condition. That comes in Act 5, when Azdek the judge orders the child Michael to be placed in the centre of a chalk circle and decrees that he will belong to the woman who pulls him out of the circle first.

Natella, the governor’s wife and the birth mother who abandoned the child pulls him out, twice. Grusha, the servant girl who saved him at the risk of her life, refuses to pull him, for she can’t bear to hurt him. Natella wants something from the child (access to her dead husband’s properties); Grusha wants something for the child, a life lived as a compassionate human being and not a brutal oppressor.

Sri Lanka is that child now, caught between many Natellas with no Grusha in sight. Unlike Michael, we bear considerable responsibility for our own plight. Gota must go home, for he is the chief author of our tragedy. But he sits right because he can cling to the massive mandate we gave him. We elected a silly little showman with a Hitler complex and now we are saddled with him.
Rejecting all 225 parliamentarians is a fad now, but other than national list members, everyone else of that despised tribe were also elected by us.

Chamal Rajapaksa rightly faulted brother Mahinda for not bowing out of politics after his defeat at the 2015 Presidential election. Had he done so, there would have been no Mahinda Sulanga, no SLPP, no President Gotabaya. Sri Lanka would have muddled along as she did for 72 years, with some successes and many failures. Rajapaksa greed brought us all to this pass, but it was we the people who gave them the chance to wreck Sri Lanka. Understanding our own culpability is necessary to prevent a repeat. If we don’t realise and accept the role we played in our own downfall, we will elect another set of venal incompetents at the next election.

Gota won’t go and there’s no constitutional path to oust him fast, so the only option is to defang him via a 21st Amendment and to hold a referendum on the presidential system in tandem with the next parliamentary election. But there’s a kind of a use value in Gota refusing to go home. So long as he clings to the presidency, people will remember the true authors of their unbearable suffering. If he’s gone, the role of the Rajapaksas will be forgotten gradually, and the blame heaped on whoever is governing, be it Ranil, Sajith, Anura or anyone else.

After a few months of non-Rajapaksa rule, Mahinda Rajapaksa will claim that he had a plan, but couldn’t implement it due to national and international conspiracies. And quite a few of those who are now clamouring for Gota’s departure now will scratch their collective heads then muttering, That is also true (Ekath aththa).

There might not be another Mahinda Sulanga but a Mahinda Madanala (Mahinda Breeze) is a distinct possibility under such circumstances. Allowing a defanged Gotabaya Rajapaksa to cling to the presidency until the worst of the crisis is over might make political sense, given Lankan public’s well known capacity to swing from one extreme to another in short oder.
In the final act of the Caucasian Chalk Circle, a song reflecting Grusha’s hopes for the child ends with the line that she wants him to fear darkness but not the night.
Last night, the house of an IOC fuel station owner in Kekirawa was torched, allegedly by people who could not purchase fuel. That is our darkness.
Yesterday, an internet post reported that in Walgama, residents provided those in a fuel queue overnight with biscuits, tea, and even breakfast.

We need dollars urgently. We also need to find and cling to ordinary everyday decency, kindness, and empathy that are in us. We can either become Grushas and be kind to each other or turn into Natellas and tear each other apart.
